childNodes.textContent: A little pet for the trace column
Give the long agent-loop wait some life — without taking screen space
A little pet for the trace column

node.textContent: Watching a long run is a lonely wait.
The trace column just ticks rows by while the agent thinks, tests run, and a fix lands. Nothing on the screen is alive. A small ambient companion can add warmth to that wait — and cost nothing.
anchored to .iv__trace, never displaces a row
bobs, blinks, wanders, the occasional snack & poop
off by default; one toggle turns it on
inline SVG + CSS keyframes — no images, no deps, no network
A small animated SVG pet at the trace-column bottom
Absolutely positioned — never blocks or displaces trace rows
Pure inline SVG + CSS — no images, deps, or network
A published PRD and an HTML mockup of the pet in the real drive layout — the validated product output that the architect picks up next.
